exemplar.title: To what extent has GrabFoodâs innovation been an effective growth strategy to increase its market share in Southeast Asia?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has GrabFoodâs innovation been an effective growth strategy to increase its market share in Southeast Asia?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly stated and well-reinforced topic in title and introduction
Research question focused on a single firm, strategy and outcome with visible link to discussion
Appropriate range of secondary sources with acknowledgement of limitations
weaknesses: Lacks concise justification of significance within IB Business context
Methodology relies solely on secondary data without primary triangulation
Limited critical rationale for selecting some sources over others
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation communicates its topic and research question accurately and maintains focus throughout, placing it solidly in the top half of the 3â4 band. A broader, more explicit justification for the studyâs significance and a deeper critical explanation of source selection would push it to the very top. The score of 5 reflects effective communication and focus, with room for deeper methodological justification.

strengths: Consistent and relevant application of quantitative data (GMV, revenue charts, market-share maps)
Clear and coherent demonstration of subject knowledge, integrating Ansoff, Porterâs Five Forces and marketing mix
Accurate and mostly consistent use of IB Business terminology
weaknesses: Occasional unsupported assertions about customer behaviour
Minor terminology slips (e.g. âfirst mobile advantageâ)
Some images are decorative rather than analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work applies source material clearly and coherently, demonstrating strong business knowledge and consistent use of relevant terminology. While a few statements lack direct evidence and there are minor terminology errors, the overall quality meets the 5â6 band. Awarding 6 acknowledges very strong performance with only a few small areas for improvement.

strengths: Logical use of analytical tools (Ansoff, Porterâs) to structure argumentation
Balanced and well-developed conclusion acknowledging both benefits and risks of innovation
Purposeful selection of recent, relevant quantitative data across multiple competitors
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive without advanced metrics (e.g. CAGR, ratios)
Evaluation often tool-by-tool rather than fully synthesised
Limitations section identifies constraints but lacks depth on potential bias impact
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear analysis with well-supported conclusions and a reasoned, critical argument, placing performance at the top of the 7â9 band. The absence of sophisticated quantitative metrics and deeper evaluation of methodological biases prevents entry into the highest band. A score of 9 rewards strong critical thinking with targeted areas for enhancement.

strengths: Fully compliant structure: title page with word count, contents, numbered sections and bibliography
Consistent formatting: font, spacing, heading hierarchy and citation style
Effective integration and referencing of visuals
weaknesses: Some figures lack axis labels or clear legends
Minor inconsistencies in bibliography styling and inline numbering
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all IB requirements with a clear, logical structure and professional formatting. Minor visual labelling and style inconsistencies do not detract from readability, justifying full marks in this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is presented verbatim in the introduction and is focused on one firm, one strategy (innovation in GrabFood) and one outcome (market-share growth in SEA).  It is sufficiently narrow to be answered within 4,000 words, and the link between the RQ and the ensuing discussion is visible throughout the body.  Nevertheless, phrases like âto what extentâ¦innovation been an effective growth strategyâ still encompass several dimensions (GMV, revenue, market cap) that are not always integrated, so the focus is only partially sharp, placing it securely in the 3-4 band rather than 5-6.

The essay culminates in a balanced conclusion that recognises both the success of innovation and its cost-effectiveness risks.  Earlier sections include evaluation of each Porter force and limitations table, demonstrating some critical stance.  However, evaluation is often tool-by-tool rather than synthesised, and criteria for judging âeffectivenessâ are not rigorously weighted.  Argumentation is therefore well-developed but only partially critical.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of growth-strategy theory (Ansoff), marketing mix, Porterâs Five Forces and integrates factual detail about Grabâs operations.  The narrative remains coherent and business-focused.  However, some explanations merely restate textbook definitions before linking to Grab, and depth of insight into financial metrics is limited, so the use of sources is competent rather than fully âeffectiveâ.

Key business terms â GMV, market penetration, value-added pricing â are used correctly and consistently.  Minor slips (e.g. âfirst mobile advantageâ instead of âfirst-moverâ) appear but do not impede understanding.  The consistent deployment of IB Business vocabulary places the work solidly in the 3-4 band.

Analytical tools are applied logically; for example, the Ansoff discussion traces Grabâs shift from product development to diversification with supporting evidence, and revenue data are interpreted to link innovation spending to growth.  Nonetheless, numerical analysis remains mostly descriptive (no ratios, CAGR or statistical comparison) and some conclusions (e.g. economies of scale claims) are asserted without full calculation.  This represents clear, well-supported analysis but not the sophistication required for higher bands.

Most data used (GMV tables, revenue charts, market-share maps) are integrated directly into the analysis of Grabâs performance.  Citations are generally accurate and relevant; the majority originate from credible industry or company filings.  Occasional unsupported assertions (e.g. claims about customer behaviour without evidence) and a few decorative images weaken the overall application, keeping the work within the mid-band.

A range of secondary sources is consulted (annual reports, Momentum Works reports, academic articles) and several analytical tools are selected.  The essay explains why secondary data were chosen and recognises limitations.  However, there is no primary research and some sources (blogs, press releases) are of uneven academic quality.  Evidence of conscious, critical selection is present but only intermittently explained; hence the methodology is adequate rather than clearly âeffective and informedâ.

The student amasses a broad range of recent quantitative data (GMV by country, revenue trends, market cap) that are directly relevant to the RQ.  Sources span 2019-24 and cover multiple competitors, indicating purposeful selection.  Absence of primary data and reliance on company-issued figures limit claims to âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ research, warranting the top of the 4-6 band.

Formatting is clean: consistent font, heading hierarchy, double-spacing, numbered pages and correctly formatted tables/figures.  Citations follow a single style reasonably consistently.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g. bold vs. italic in bibliography) do not detract from overall professional layout, justifying full marks in this strand.

The student states the topic explicitly in the title and reinforces it in the introduction, providing background on Grab, the scope (Southeast Asia) and the indicators that will be examined (market-share %, GMV, revenue).  The explanation is largely clear and gives enough contextual information for an unfamiliar reader to understand the focus of the study.  Minor omissions â such as a concise justification of why the topic is significant in the IB Business context â prevent this from reaching the comprehensive clarity expected of the top band, but overall the communication is adequate and generally effective.

The essay follows EE conventions: title page with word count, formal table of contents, clearly labelled sections, correctly sequenced pages, bibliography.  Visuals are integrated and referenced.  All structural elements stipulated by the IB guide are present and logically organised, fulfilling the criteria for the top presentation band.

annotations.comment: The title clearly states the focus on GrabFood innovation and market-share growth, effectively orienting the reader to the investigationâs scope.
Word count is provided and under the 4000-word limit, meeting IB formatting requirements and ensuring compliance.
Table of contents is comprehensive and logically organized, enabling easy navigation through sections and sub-sections.
Introduction gives historical and operational context but would benefit from a concise justification of why GrabFoodâs innovation is significant for strategic growth.
Figure lacks axis labels or a clear legend. Include units and a descriptive title in the visual itself for clarity.
The methodology section lists tools and sources but offers limited critical justification for why these were selected over alternatives.
Methodology relies solely on secondary data. Consider triangulating with primary insights (e.g. expert interviews) to strengthen research quality.
Limitations section identifies constraints but lacks analytical depth on how each limitation might bias findings.
The research question is clear but could be strengthened by explicitly stating the gap it addresses and its broader strategic importance.
Stating that the RQ arose from personal use introduces subjectivity; clarify methodological rationale for formulating the question.
Table header uses empty cells, making columns unclear. Label each column explicitly to improve readability.
The Ansoff Matrix explanation is overly generic; integrate specific GMV or market-share data to illustrate each quadrantâs relevance.
Analysis of GrabFoodâs shift to diversification is clear, but lacks quantitative metrics (e.g. CAGR) to support growth claims.
Terminology slip: âfirst mobile advantageâ should read âfirst-mover advantage.â Consistent use of correct terms enhances precision.
Pricing discussion asserts effectiveness but offers no comparative fee data. Include actual delivery-fee figures to substantiate claims.
Inline math used for page number is inconsistent with text style. Ensure uniform formatting of page indicators.
Describes AI-prompted replies without defining term or source. Elaborate on technology and cite a reference.
Promotion section details tactics but does not evaluate ROI or customer-acquisition metrics. Incorporate evaluation data.
Porterâs Five Forces intro defines tool but needs direct linkage to specific SEA market data for each force.
The Threat of New Entrants section offers cost detail but would be enriched by comparing these with competitorsâ R&D spend.
The application of sunk-cost effect to buyer loyalty is well explained and demonstrates good use of consumer psychology.
Annual revenue chart lacks clear axis labels and unit markers. Add detailed axis titles to improve interpretability.
âFirst mobile advantageâ appears again in figure discussion. Use consistent, correct terminology throughout.
Table 2 presents competitive factors but omits discussion of how commission-rate differences impact partner behaviour and revenue.
Conclusion recaps findings well but does not integrate earlier limitations. Link limitations directly to final evaluative remarks.
Bibliography entries vary in use of italics and bold. Apply a consistent citation style to improve professionalism.
